summary: Expose the complete Agent Workbench BFF projection contract to the React API client.
rationale: The Workbench React app must depend only on BFF routes, and every visible field must be backed by BFF response or stream metadata. This PR changes the BFF route/model/projection contract together with the generated OpenAPI schema and typed React client boundary so intermediate stack branches remain buildable.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
deletion_targets: React calls to non-BFF conversation creation routes
frontend-visible Workbench fields without BFF response or stream support
manual typed client normalization for retired non-Workbench conversation create responses
risks: BFF response models and generated OpenAPI schema must remain in sync.
Stream projection metadata must preserve round, lane, phase, and status fields used by React.
The React API client must not regain access to non-Workbench API paths.
